perturbation.original_text: As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with some, but not necessarily all, of the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply with some, but not necessarily all, obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "strictly" and changing "all" to "some, but not necessarily all" this introduces ambiguity and contradicts the overall intent of the agreement, creating uncertainty in how thoroughly the Confidential Information needs to be protected. This contradicts section 3, which speaks about limitations on the Confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to treat confidential information 'strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement' and to comply with 'all obligations'. The changed text weakens this by stating the Receiving Party only needs to comply with 'some, but not necessarily all, of the provisions' and 'some, but not necessarily all, obligations'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever'. The changed text allows for selective compliance, directly conflicting with the original, stricter requirement to keep all Confidential Information safe and not disclose any of it.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession prior to disclosure by the Company, provided that such information, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, was not subject to another confidentiality agreement with the Company or another party, (c) has been independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to, or the use of, any Confidential Information or (d) is disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, not bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that the Receiving Party claims (a) is or becomes available to the public, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) has been independently developed by the Receiving Party or (d) is disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'can demonstrate', 'other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement', 'provided that such information, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, was not subject to another confidentiality agreement with the Company or another party', 'without reference to, or the use of, any Confidential Information' and 'to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, not bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis' and by changing "claims" removes the burden of proof and the limitations on the exceptions. This creates a contradiction with the limitations in section 3, allowing potentially broad claims to exclude information, weakening the core confidentiality protections. It removes the qualifications and requirements that ensured these exceptions are valid, making the receiving party's claim alone sufficient. This contradicts Section 3 which places limitations on the use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the confidentiality protections by removing the burden of proof ('can demonstrate') and limitations on the exceptions to what constitutes confidential information. Specifically, removing phrases like 'other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in breach of the terms of this Agreement', 'provided that such information, to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, was not subject to another confidentiality agreement with the Company or another party', 'without reference to, or the use of, any Confidential Information' and 'to the Receiving Party’s knowledge, not bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis' from the exceptions in the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts the limitations imposed on the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information in the contradicted section. The contradicted section states the Receiving Party must keep the Confidential Information in confidence and use it solely for evaluating a transaction, but the modified text allows broader claims to exclude information from being considered Confidential Information, therefore creating a contradiction, because if something isn't classified as Confidential Information, the Receiving Party doesn't have to keep it confidential under the terms of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, make reasonable effort to keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "keep the Confidential Information in confidence" to "make reasonable effort to keep the Confidential Information in confidence" and removing the third exception, the certainty of non-disclosure is weakened. The removal of any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives creates uncertainty in to whom information can be disclosed. This creates a contradiction within Section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, make reasonable effort to keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'keep the Confidential Information in confidence' to 'make reasonable effort to keep the Confidential Information in confidence' weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity where the original text established a clearer standard. Furthermore, the removal of the third exception regarding disclosure to the Receiving Party's Representatives creates uncertainty about permissible disclosures. Since the Contradicted Text still states that the Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, make reasonable effort to keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, the changed text contradicts the original text.

perturbation.original_text: (b) The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any employee of the Company
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clauses that specify which employees, by changing "any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring." to "any employee of the Company", the restriction is broad and ambiguous. It creates a contradiction with 3a and section 22.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary provided elsewhere herein, none of the provisions of this Agreement shall in any way limit the ordinary course business services and activities of the Receiving Party and its affiliates; provided, that (a) such services and activities are distinct from the private equity business and (b) the Confidential Information is not made available to Representatives of the Receiving Party and its affiliates who are not involved in the private equity business or who are engaged in investments that are not private equity investments and such ordinary course business services and activities are otherwise conducted without any reference to, or use of, the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party further represents to the Company that the Receiving Party has implemented appropriate internal restrictions on the sharing of confidential information (including, without limitation, the implementation of ethical walls around certain affiliates, to comply with federal securities laws of the United States).
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the non-solicitation clause to senior employees and those met during the transaction evaluation, explicitly allowing general advertisements. The changed text broadens the non-solicitation clause to *any* employee, removing the exceptions. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which allows the Receiving Party's ordinary course business activities without limit, provided the confidential information isn't used. Soliciting employees could be considered part of the Receiving Party's ordinary business, thus conflicting with the broadened non-solicitation clause in the Changed Text. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return the Confidential Information to the Company, and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain Confidential Information shall be destroyed. (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Promptly" and "written" from 'Promptly upon the written request', as well as "all", "notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other", and the two "Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof." the company's control and the clarity on the terms of data retention is reduced. This creates a contradiction with the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3, making enforcement harder.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the obligations of the Receiving Party regarding the return and destruction of Confidential Information. By removing 'Promptly', 'written', 'all', 'notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other', and the terms relating to continued confidentiality, as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation, the changed text reduces the company's control over its confidential information, creating a contradiction with the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3 (Contradicted Text). Specifically, it's now less clear how and when the Receiving Party must return or destroy information, which contradicts the requirement to 'keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever'.
